introduction: A state of subnormal concentration of thyroid hormones in the circulation is called hypothyroidism. The extreme form of hypothyroidism is referred to as myxoedema.

features: Dull facial expression, slow speech and poor memory
Puffiness of the hands, feet and face
Thinning, dryness and loss of hair
Bradycardia, reduced systolic and increased diastolic blood pressure

investigations: Total serum T3 and T4 levels

drug: Levothyroxine sodium (thyroxine sodium) for adults and children with specific dosages
Liothyronine sodium (1-tri-iodothyronine sodium) for adults and children with specific dosages
adverse_reactions_and_cautions: Thyroxine should not be used alone for long term replacement therapy
Monitor serum levels of hormones to ensure that patients are not exposed to cardiac risks
supportive_measures: Treat anaemia, constipation and other complications as appropriate
Immediate mechanical ventilation in myxoedema coma

complications: Myxoedema coma in adults and cretinism in the young
prevention: Iodinated salt to prevent iodine deficiency
